oracle_b.reason: arm = worktrees; Oracle B scoped to maw refs

transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# git-worktrees-bare arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **plain git worktrees plus a hand-rolled convention**. The repo is a bare repo at `<root>/repo.git/` with worktrees checked out under sibling directories. The default integration target is the `main` worktree (typically `<root>/main/`). `git` is on `$PATH`; there is no maw, no jj.

## Core verbs

- `git worktree add ../<name> -b <name>` — make a new worktree on a fresh branch.
- `git worktree list` — list all worktrees.
- `git worktree remove <name>` — remove a worktree's checkout (the branch ref stays unless you also `git branch -D <name>`).
- `git fetch origin` and `git rebase origin/main` (or `git pull --rebase`) — bring a stale worktree up to the latest integration head.
- `git diff <branch>..HEAD` — show a worktree's changes vs the integration head.
- `git checkout main && git merge --no-ff <a> [b ...]` — merge worktree branches into `main`. On conflict, `git status` shows the marker-laden files; resolve manually then `git add` + `git commit`. `git merge --abort` rolls back.
- `git push` / `git pull` — sync with the bare repo when applicable.

## The coordination convention

Without a coordinator the agents must follow a written convention to avoid stepping on each other:

- **Claim files**: before starting work on a workspace, write a small file like `.coord/claims/<name>.json` recording who owns it and when. Release it (delete or mark released) when done.
- **Merge ordering**: integrate one workspace at a time into `main`. Never merge two unrelated worktrees in parallel.
- **Stale worktrees**: rebase onto `origin/main` (or `main`) before requesting a merge.
- **Recovery**: deleted worktree branches still live in `git reflog` and the bare repo's object store. Use `git reflog show <branch>` and `git checkout <sha>` to restore a lost branch.
- **Cleanup**: after `git worktree remove`, run `git worktree prune` to clear stale metadata.

The full convention is the document at `notes/sg2-worktrees-convention.md` (and equivalent in this substrate). Read it before coordinating.

## Conflicts

There is no "conflict as data" behavior here — `git rebase` and `git merge` abort or stop on conflict and require manual resolution. Resolve files in place, `git add` them, then `git rebase --continue` or `git commit`.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- The coordination convention document.
- `git worktree --help`.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS
Scenario seed: 14954182755739252794

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).
3. Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.
4. Remove workspace `ws-1`.

- `git fetch origin` and `git rebase origin/main` (or `git pull --rebase`) — bring a stale worktree up to the latest integration head.
- `git diff <branch>..HEAD` — show a worktree's changes vs the integration head.

turns.reply_text: I'll explore the workspace structure first, then complete the task battery.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"ls /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/ && echo \"---\" && ls /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/repo.git/ 2>/dev/null || echo \"no repo.git\"","description":"Explore workspace structure"}

main
repo.git
---
config
description
HEAD
hooks
info
objects
refs

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/repo.git && git worktree list && echo \"---\" && git branch -a && echo \"---\" && git log --oneline -5 2>/dev/null || echo \"no commits\"","description":"Check worktrees, branches, and git log"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/repo.git && git worktree list --verbose","description":"List all worktrees with details"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"ls /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main/ && cat /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main/.git 2>/dev/null || echo \"no .git file\"","description":"Check main worktree contents"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main && git log --oneline -3 && echo \"---\" && git status","description":"Check main worktree git state"}

b13eea4 init
---
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.

nothing to commit, working tree clean

turns.reply_text:
Good. Now I'll complete the task battery:

**Task 1: Create workspace `ws-0` based on the default branch (main)**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main && git worktree add ../ws-0 -b ws-0 && echo \"Created ws-0\"","description":"Create ws-0 worktree on new branch ws-0"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Preparing worktree (new branch 'ws-0')
HEAD is now at b13eea4 init
Created ws-0

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main && git worktree list","description":"Verify ws-0 was created"}

turns.reply_text: **Task 2: Remove workspace `ws-0` (force, even if uncommitted work)**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main && git worktree remove --force ../ws-0 && echo \"Removed ws-0\" && git worktree prune && echo \"Pruned stale metadata\"","description":"Force-remove ws-0 worktree and prune metadata"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main && git worktree list && echo \"---\" && git branch -a && echo \"---\" && ls /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/","description":"Verify ws-0 worktree removed; branch ws-0 still exists"}

/tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main b13eea4 [main]
---

  ws-0
  remotes/origin/main
---
main
repo.git

turns.reply_text:
The worktree checkout was removed but branch `ws-0` still lives in the repo. 

**Task 3: Recover `ws-0` into new workspace `ws-1`**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main && git worktree add ../ws-1 -b ws-1 ws-0 && echo \"Created ws-1 from ws-0\"","description":"Create ws-1 worktree branching from ws-0 to recover its state"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Preparing worktree (new branch 'ws-1')
HEAD is now at b13eea4 init
Created ws-1 from ws-0

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main && git worktree list && ls /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/","description":"Verify ws-1 exists"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main && git worktree remove --force ../ws-1 && git worktree prune && echo \"Removed ws-1 and pruned\" && git worktree list && ls /tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/","description":"Force-remove ws-1 worktree and prune"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Removed ws-1 and pruned
/tmp/.tmpLkEoSS/main b13eea4 [main]
main
repo.git

turns.reply_text:
All four tasks complete. Here's a summary of what was done:

1. **Created `ws-0`**: `git worktree add ../ws-0 -b ws-0` — new branch `ws-0` from `main` HEAD.
2. **Removed `ws-0`**: `git worktree remove --force ../ws-0` — deleted the checkout directory; branch ref `ws-0` was intentionally left in the repo to enable recovery.
3. **Recovered `ws-0` into `ws-1`**: `git worktree add ../ws-1 -b ws-1 ws-0` — created new branch `ws-1` starting from `ws-0`'s commit, checked out into a new worktree at `../ws-1`.
4. **Removed `ws-1`**: `git worktree remove --force ../ws-1` + `git worktree prune`.

Substrate is clean — only the `main` worktree remains.

**done**
